claim: Foundation models can detect card testing by converting each transaction into a dense embedding that reveals hidden patterns across large merchant volumes, improving detection from ~59% to ~97% on large accounts by catching clusters of small test transactions that traditional ML misses.
actionable: When building fraud detection for payments at scale, use foundation model embeddings to capture sequential context of transactions rather than isolated statistical features.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Free trial abuse and refund abuse are existential threats to AI companies because GPU inference costs are high; this differs fundamentally from SaaS where marginal cost of serving was near-zero, making fraud profitability economics work differently.
actionable: If monetizing AI services via free trials or subscriptions, implement specialized fraud detection and outcome-based billing to protect unit economics from non-payment abuse.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Token billing allows AI companies building on top of LLM providers to track and price to real-time inference costs; when upstream model costs drop 80% or rise 3x, companies can dynamically adjust pricing without destroying unit economics.
actionable: Implement outcome-based or usage-based token billing rather than fixed subscriptions if your service depends on upstream LLM costs that fluctuate.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: The Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP) is a shared standard for agents to query products, pricing, and brand from merchants; it decouples from specific payment provider or LLM provider, allowing merchants to integrate once and work with any agent.
actionable: When building agent-to-merchant integrations, design as a protocol first to avoid lock-in and enable the merchant to serve many agents with a single integration.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: AI companies are 2-3x faster than SaaS companies at reaching million/10M/30M ARR milestones, are twice as global at year-end (55+ countries), and show retention churn within the AI cohort rather than from AI to non-AI, indicating high competitive density in product categories.
actionable: When analyzing AI company metrics, compare against baseline SaaS cohorts from 5 years prior to account for acceleration; monitor within-cohort churn as a signal of competitive market health rather than product failure.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Outcome-based pricing (e.g., pay only for resolved support cases) reduces buyer friction for enterprise adoption because it shifts the risk from the buyer evaluating unproven AI to the AI company guaranteeing value in concrete business terms.
actionable: For enterprise-facing AI products, define a measurable business outcome (cost saved, cases resolved, defects prevented) and price as a function of that outcome rather than seats or usage.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Stablecoins as payment method remove international card costs (4-12 percentage points) and can shift 20% of transaction volume, making them valuable for high-cost, global AI companies; 50% of stablecoin volume is incremental revenue.
actionable: For AI products with high price points and global reach, enable stablecoin payment as an alternative to international cards to recover margin and attract web3-native users.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: LLM-driven feature engineering and integration work can compress timelines (2 weeks vs. 2 months for payment method integrations); however, measure productivity gains over 2-3 years rather than immediate term because model quality improves and costs fluctuate.
actionable: When justifying AI coding tools to leadership, defend on 2-3 year ROI curves rather than quarter-to-quarter metrics; use model swaps (GPT-4 → GPT-4o → o3-mini) as case studies of improving cost-to-capability.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Claimable sandboxes (pre-configured Stripe test environments embeddable in dev tools like Vercel/Replit) reduce new-business creation friction; sandbox can be claimed to go live without re-authentication, enabling non-technical founders to build monetized products.
actionable: If building a platform for AI startups or low-code creators, embed pre-configured, claimable sandboxes directly in the IDE/builder rather than requiring users to leave the flow to set up payments.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Data discovery is the bottleneck for text-to-SQL on internal data; LLMs perform worst at finding the right table/field, not at writing the query; improve by documenting high-quality canonical datasets and using organizational context (e.g., 'I work on LPM data').
actionable: When deploying LLM-based analytics (text-to-SQL), prioritize semantic layer documentation and canonical dataset curation over prompt engineering; add user context (team/domain) as implicit prompt guidance.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Fraud detection should operate on multiple signals upstream of payment processing (sign-up, usage patterns, behavioral) and should classify not just fraudulent disputes but suspicious transactions that don't result in chargebacks but harm the business.
actionable: Design fraud systems to block before checkout rather than only at charge time; use foundation models to flag suspicious non-dispute transactions and provide human-readable reasoning for appeals.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Large-scale LLM costs can be justified by long-term headcount savings; o3-mini (cheaper, more capable than GPT-4o) replaced human decision-makers in risk tasks, saving $3M/year; model improvements + cost drops compound, justifying today's investment.
actionable: When deciding to adopt a more expensive LLM-based system, build a headcount replacement model and track quarterly; expect 2-4 quarter payoff as models improve and commodity alternatives emerge.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Agentic commerce expands consumption by high-income individuals by removing time friction; if agents handle shopping discovery and checkout, high-income people (who spend lower share of income due to time cost) become incremental revenue, not substitution.
actionable: When evaluating agents as new revenue channels, distinguish between substitution (existing customers buying differently) and expansion (new customer segments due to friction removal); model expansion via time-cost economics.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Brand and user experience matter as much as technology in AI companies; differentiation comes from design, community, and compelling UX (e.g., Lovable, Cursor) rather than raw model capability; invest in design excellence alongside research.
actionable: If building an AI product, allocate design and brand resources equally with engineering/research; focus on user delight and community building as primary competitive moats.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: Build vs. Buy decision: Always start with buy, then spotlight program (RFP-based evaluation), and only build if market solutions don't exist; once launched, re-evaluate quarterly whether to swap in external solutions as market matures.
actionable: Establish a quarterly review cycle where internal AI tools/platforms are re-evaluated for external alternatives; maintain capability to switch to open-source or commercial solutions without sunk-cost trap.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe

claim: AI companies pricing to seat-based models risk death spirals (AI replaces workers → fewer seats → less revenue); instead price to usage-based, outcome-based, or token-based models so revenue is decoupled from headcount replaced.
actionable: Avoid pricing AI products per-user or per-seat; instead tie pricing to input tokens consumed, outcomes delivered, or value generated so the product's efficiency doesn't undermine its revenue model.
source_title: The Agents Economy Backbone - with Emily Glassberg Sands, Head of Data & AI at Stripe
